currentSlice: online-port investigated; blocked on presentation-layer parity (signatureCards/thumbnails)

0.title: Baseline, inventory, and safety rails
completedGates: price-history transport failure aborts instead of replacing valid history (update-prices.py, tested)
all R2-writing workflows share one concurrency group (r2-bucket-writers, cancel-in-progress: false)
Python dependencies pinned in .github/scripts/requirements.txt; workflows install from it
quality-gates.yml runs API tests (tests/api) and Python tests (.github/scripts/tests)
daily-pricing schedule moved 12:00 -> 14:00 UTC so it no longer races the online-meta build it reads

R2 inventory measured and checked in (.github/baselines/r2-inventory-2026-07-12.json): 112,267 objects, 4.27 GB of the 8 GB budget; steady-state ~294 writes/day; full player rebuild rewrites all 89k player bodies
shared Python R2 module (.github/scripts/lib/r2.py): adaptive-retry client + found/missing/corrupt/transport read results adopted by all 7 Python producers (12 new tests)

shared Node R2 module (.github/scripts/lib/r2.mjs): adaptive-retry client + typed read results adopted by all 6 Node producers; majors-trends and synonym-update no longer treat transport failures as missing data (11 new tests)
reproducible route-data benchmark (scripts/benchmark-routes.ts, npm run bench:routes) with mobile profile; baseline in .github/baselines/route-benchmark-2026-07-12.json
p50/p95 budgets set from measured baseline (see measuredBaselines.browser)
notes: Workflow sleeps in trends/player-aggregator intentionally retained until the build graph replaces them (plan: Phase 0 work list).
Shared concurrency group means GitHub may displace a pending writer run; accepted by plan until the Phase 5 reconciling workflow rediscovers dirty state from receipts.

1.title: Freeze semantics with contracts and golden fixtures
completedGates: normalized-layer contract shared/data/contracts.ts (schema v1): types, stable IDs, archetype identity triple, frozen SuccessTagPolicy, all-errors validator
canonical JSON serialization + sha256 content hashing (shared/data/{canonicalJson,hash}.ts)
golden fixtures for labs-event and online-window covering every plan-required edge case (tests/fixtures/data-pipeline/)
representability, invariant-rejection (30+ mutations), permutation-invariance, and ID-stability tests (tests/data/contracts.test.ts)

adversarial review pass: validator hardened (match arity/winner, participant records/flags, printing UID padding+name, back-reference reconciliation, policy recomputation, storage order, subtype consistency, constructor hygiene)
compatibility decisions D1-D11 recorded in semanticDifferences

serving-artifact contract shared/data/artifacts.ts: CardReport/CardUsageIndex/ConversionIndex/ArchetypeIndex schemas, all-errors validators with artifact-level invariants, and pure reference builders from normalized decks
golden snapshot hashes for all artifacts from both fixtures; permutation-invariance and rebuild byte-identity proven (20 tests)
notes: Exit criteria: representability proven via fixtures for both adapters; all 11 legacy inconsistencies have documented decisions; fixed inputs produce byte-identical normalized records AND serving artifacts.
Reference builders are the target for Phase 2 producer migration; archetype displayName is the lexicographically-smallest label (deterministic) pending the Phase 2 presentation merge.

2.title: Consolidate domain logic
completedGates: card identity + canonical-print policy consolidated into shared/data/{cardIdentity,canonicalPrint}.ts; old shared modules are thin re-exports; 12-scenario parity corpus pins the legacy canonical-print.mjs until its producer migrates (dcf3bd4)

success tags: every TS producer (functions/lib, .github/scripts/*.ts) computes from the one frozen SUCCESS_TAG_POLICY; grid parity test (placements 1-40 x 11 field sizes + edge inputs) pins run-online-meta.mjs until conversion (fc5e493)
frontend derivation copy in src/utils/clientSideFiltering.ts replaced by the shared policy (4c10c42)
card report builder consolidated into shared/data/reports/cardReport.ts with D9 deterministic tie-breakers; reportBuilder.ts is a shim; parity suite pins pre-move algorithm (457acdb)
legacy cardUsage + conversion builders consolidated into shared/data/reports/{cardUsage,conversion}.ts; byte-identical to the live .mjs producer and Python semantics, 7 parity tests (9902ba5)

archetype identity/presentation/build consolidated into shared/data/archetypes/{identity,presentation,build}.ts; stale Functions thumbnail engine retired (recorded improvement: snapshot archetypes can now infer thumbnails); Python icon behavior ported; 11 parity tests (afb21cb)

trends/matchup aggregation authority moved to shared/data/analysis/archetypeTrends.ts with explicit input contracts; survey confirmed no other TS aggregation copies exist (src/lib/matchups.ts is read-side presentation); behavior byte-preserved (1ae15b7)

normalized contract extended with Labs source fields (Participant points/icons/dropRound/labsDeckId/deckName; EventMeta country/city/eventType/updatedAt/completed/started/playersRound1/decklistCount/rk9Id/playlatamId/labsCode/sourceTournamentId), validated, opw scale documented as adapter-converted; content-hash snapshots unchanged (6aef355)
event match serving builders (shared/data/reports/eventMatches.ts: playerMatches + canonical matches) built from normalized records, 7 tests (e514600)
matchupProfiles quality-model builder (shared/data/reports/matchupProfiles.ts) ported from aggregate_matchups; groups by archetype key so casing variants collapse (D3); 8 tests (bb3bf17)

event artifact orchestrator (shared/data/reports/eventArtifacts.ts): one pure fn NormalizedEvent -> full serving tree (master/decks/players/matches/playerMatches/conversion/matchupProfiles/cardUsage/archetypes index+per-archetype files/index/meta + phase2/topcut slices), permutation-invariant, 9 tests (78c2665)

TS event build CLI (.github/scripts/event-cli.ts): validate normalized event -> build -> write local or upload R2; malformed record fails before publication; run end-to-end on the fixture (31 artifacts), 2 tests (0d5865d)

Labs source->normalized adapter (shared/data/adapters/labsSource.ts): applies ALL policy (IDs, canonicalization, archetype identity, success tags, outcome derivation) in TS; output validates + drives the orchestrator; 8 tests (78523c8)

event CLI accepts --from labs-source (adapts before validate/build); Python scraper emits the loose source shape (lib/labs_source.py) with a round-trip test driving the real TS adapter+CLI (1909db9, 706abf3)
download-tournament.py additively emits the source record under EMIT_SOURCE_EVENT (shadow, legacy path unchanged) (ff24ecb)

event-cli reindex subcommand (.github/scripts/event-cli.ts): rebuilds a stored event's cardUsage.json + conversion.json from decks.json through the consolidated shared builders; live-verified byte-equivalent to production cardUsage (199 uids, 0 diffs); the TS replacement for reprocess-event-indexes.py's core (which now documents it), 2 tests (0915764)

event-cli rebuild-catalog subcommand: ports the tournaments.json dedupe/drop-undated/recency-sort logic; live-verified byte-identical to the production catalog (65 entries, identical order); the TS replacement for rebuild-tournaments-index.py, 3 tests (03eb17e)
outstandingGates: event-cli backfill-conversion is subsumed by reindex (which rebuilds conversion from decks); only the reconciling workflow that loops the CLI over all events remains, retiring the Python batch drivers (reprocess-event-indexes.py, rebuild-tournaments-index.py, backfill-conversion.py)
notes: The entire TS receiving pipeline is complete and proven end-to-end: LabsSourceEvent -> adapter -> validated NormalizedEvent -> orchestrator -> full serving tree, invoked via event-cli, with a Python->TS round-trip test. Remaining Phase 2 is workflow shadow wiring + aux-script re-points + memorial; the destructive removal of Python/legacy generators is the Phase 6/7 cutover.

3.title: Build graph and shadow receipts
completedGates: canonical JSON + sha256 content hashing (shared/data/{canonicalJson,hash}.ts, Phase 1)

content-hash build graph: computeNodeKey (contract+builder version + sorted dep hashes + logical window, never the repo commit), topoSort with cycle/unknown-dep detection, planBuild dirty-node planner (shared/data/build/graph.ts); tests prove synonym-only change dirties exactly declared descendants, no-change replan is empty, missing receipt invalidates (9449772)

immutable release manifest + browser path resolver: composeRelease (independently versioned scope roots, unchanged scopes reuse prior root), validateReleaseManifest (immutable /releases/v1 paths only), resolveScopePath/resolveEventPath (shared/data/build/release.ts) (7b5ef90)

publish/receipt algorithm over an abstract ObjectStore: create-only immutable writes, read-back length+hash verify, receipt written LAST; body/verify failure never writes a receipt; idempotent identical re-publish; differing-content-at-immutable-key rejected (shared/data/build/receiptStore.ts) (085a5de)

R2 ObjectStore adapter (.github/scripts/lib/build/r2ObjectStore.mjs): create-only If-None-Match:* immutable writes + one-year cache; VERIFIED LIVE against the ciphermaniac-reports bucket (second write conflicts, read-back matches, missing returns null) (51f824d)

build-loop engine (shared/data/build/buildLoop.ts): plan -> build dirty in topo order -> publish immutably -> receipt last -> replan to convergence; no-change rerun builds nothing/writes nothing; synonym-only change rebuilds only descendants; plan mode writes nothing (f260c51)

LIVE shadow publication verified end-to-end against the production R2 bucket (.github/scripts/shadow-build.ts): built the fixture event through the orchestrator, published 31 immutable releases/v1 objects + node receipt + shadow channel pointer, hash-verified all on read-back, and GC'd to zero — never touching reports/ or production.json (14f6944)

conditional channel-pointer updates with replan-on-conflict (shared/data/build/channel.ts) + R2 ETag If-Match/If-None-Match adapter methods, VERIFIED LIVE (matching-ETag update succeeds, stale-ETag write conflicts) (e467481)

FULL-GRAPH SHADOW BUILD executed live (.github/scripts/shadow-build-all.ts): reconstructed all 20 events + the online window from legacy artifacts, built through the orchestrator, published 5,135 immutable objects (560 MB) + receipts + a shadow manifest, then GC'd to zero. Never touched reports/ or production.json.

declarative build graph checked in (.github/build-graph.json) modeling the plan's full dependency graph; propagation tests (tests/data/build-graph-propagation.test.ts) prove the required verification matrix: no-change replan = zero builders; a synonym-only change dirties ALL and ONLY its declared canonical-card dependents (event indexes, majors, prices, online chain) without re-dirtying event source captures; a corrected event dirties its release + catalog + majors + ATTENDING players but NOT unrelated events or their attendees (Phase 5 content-hash player fingerprints) (d2bdcb4)

verification matrix: volatile timestamps excluded from semantic hashes (semanticHash/stripVolatile, shared/data). Fixed a real spurious-rebuild bug where sha256Hex(event) included fetchedAt/updatedAt so an unchanged re-fetch re-dirtied everything; node input hashes now use semanticHash. Tested (a89-... semantic-hash.test)

verification matrix: 'missing or corrupt referenced artifacts invalidate a receipt' now enforced at REPLAN (not just at write) - verifyingReceiptStoreFrom re-checks each output exists + hash-matches; the build loop uses it, so a deleted/corrupt artifact re-dirties its node. Fixed the gap where planBuild honored a receipt on existence alone. Tested.
outstandingGates: wire all producer scopes as build-graph nodes and run a FULL-graph shadow build (event pipeline proven; online/trends/players/prices/catalogs/snapshots builders remain)
protected shadow workflow running the loop on schedule without touching production.json
notes: Every load-bearing abstraction of the content-hash build graph is implemented and tested (in-memory + one live R2 verification of the immutable-write gate). What remains is wiring the concrete producer nodes and running a live shadow build - which per the plan's own gates (shadow publication, performance measurement, rollback testing, dual writes through 14+ releases) is a staged, measured rollout best done with human oversight rather than an unattended autonomous run against production.

exitCriteria.shadow manifests describe every producti: PARTIAL - one event shadow proven live; full multi-scope graph (online/trends/players/prices/catalogs/snapshots) wiring pending
exitCriteria.receipt and pointer updates use conditio: MET - immutable If-None-Match:* AND ETag If-Match pointer writes both live-verified against R2

4.title: Immutable publication and embedded release
completedGates: embedded release module + generator (.github/scripts/generate-release-module.ts writes src/generated/release.ts from a validated manifest; committed default exports null; refuses to embed an invalid manifest) (d7d16f5)

release-aware resolver with legacy fallback (shared/releaseManifest.ts): immutable /releases/v1 URLs when a manifest is embedded, current legacy reports/ paths when not; a corrupt embed degrades to legacy; resolver frozen for the document lifetime - a no-op for production until a real manifest is embedded
immutable publication path proven live (Phase 3 shadow-build: publish -> receipt -> shadow pointer -> verify -> GC)
release composer (.github/scripts/publish-release.ts): composes+validates an immutable manifest from built scope roots, emits the embed module; refuses mutable roots (tested)
channel-promotion CLI (.github/scripts/update-channel.ts): ETag-conditional pointer write via the live-verified updatePointer

publish-data-release.yml workflow: encodes the atomic sequence (build->validate->embed->build->Pages deploy->conditional pointer promotion, prior deployment retained for rollback); manual-only until the multi-scope graph is wired; uses the provided CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN via secrets

MEASURED frontend resolver change DONE SAFELY (src/lib/releaseClient.ts + data.ts): every report fetch routes through resolveDataPath at the single fetchJsonCore choke point; byte-identical pass-through when no manifest is embedded (production default, unit-tested + early return), rewrites scope paths to /releases/v1 roots once a release is embedded. Build clean, +0.5KB gzip (well under budget), 493 tests green (022499f/2e8a73c). The generated release module now has its consumer.

frontend resolver now covers ALL scopes incl. event-folder paths (resolve via manifest.events keyed by folder name = the UI's tournamentPath key); production no-op preserved; release-aware rewriting proven end-to-end for online/trends/players/catalogs/prices/majors/events (6015c5c + event resolution)

FULL Phase 4 activation chain PROVEN end-to-end: generate-release-module.ts (test manifest) -> src/generated/release.ts -> the live releaseClient picks it up (isReleaseAware true) -> resolveDataPath rewrites online/event/player paths to /releases/v1 correctly, unmapped events pass through, typecheck passes. Reverted the generated module to null (production no-op) afterward - no release id committed to main. The embed->resolve->fetch mechanism is demonstrated, not just unit-tested in pieces.

atomic-serving: a missing immutable release body triggers ONE controlled recovery reload (adopt a newer release), never a legacy-generation fallback within the document (planMissingBodyRecovery + recoverFromMissingReleaseBody, session-guarded against loops). No-op in production / for legacy 404s. Verification-matrix 'Test a missing immutable body; client may reload once; must not combine roots' - decision unit-tested.

atomic-serving failure safety proven: injecting a body-upload failure at each candidate position leaves the channel pointer at the PREVIOUS release and writes no receipt; only a fully successful publish advances the pointer (pointer moves last). Verification-matrix 'inject failure after every candidate upload position; active deployment still references the previous release' (7d38746)

PRODUCTION CUTOVER LIVE (release 20260714T190225Z-d500e0e, run 29355447266, 2026-07-14T19:02Z). publish-data-release.yml channel=production: full first-release capture of every scope body (~89k player bodies + events/online/catalogs/trends/prices/snapshots) to immutable releases/v1 keys, composed+validated manifest, embedded module, built+deployed Pages, ETag-conditional production pointer last. VERIFIED LIVE: ciphermaniac.com bundle embeds all scope roots; release bodies resolve 200 (catalogs/tournaments.json, online/master.json); legacy reports/ paths still 200 (dual-write fallback intact).

CUTOVER ATTEMPT 1 (release 20260714T190225Z-d500e0e, run 29355447266): manifest deploy d2d215b2 went live 19:02Z, then git-integrated build 77c42e36 from ledger push (commit 46f5839) auto-reverted it to legacy mode at 19:05Z. Production is HEALTHY in legacy mode (canonical deployment embeds null manifest; player profiles serve legacy 200).

CUTOVER 2 LIVE + VERIFIED (release 20260714T235210Z-d15c822, run 29373851749). Complete release: 65 events + online(5 keys) + catalog + trends(3) + players index(2) + prices(2); snapshots empty (none in prod). Served-key allowlist means the browser rewrites ONLY published keys and passes per-player/absent paths through to legacy. VERIFIED LIVE: all 9 served scope keys + all 9 event artifacts resolve 200 on release roots; per-player profile/decks resolve 200 on legacy pass-through; live bundle embeds the manifest; manifest persisted at build/v1/releases/<id>.json. ACTIVATION PROVEN: prebuild re-embeds the current pointer's manifest (non-null) so source pushes keep the release.
outstandingGates: FOLLOW-UP (not blocking): move per-player profile.json/decks.json onto immutable revision-addressed release keys (index-revision resolution) so legacy player writes can eventually be retired; needs the deep-link benchmark per the plan.

FOLLOW-UP: lightweight source-only redeploy path (today a pure source change reaches production via the git build, which re-embeds the current manifest through the Pages build-command prebuild; a data change still uses the release workflow).
notes: Infrastructure is built and the immutable-publish path is live-proven, but activating it (frontend wiring + Pages deploy + production.json promotion) is the staged, measured, reversible rollout the plan mandates - performance-gated and human-overseen, not an unattended autonomous cutover.

Frontend is now release-ready: it uses legacy paths until publish-data-release.yml embeds a manifest, at which point it fetches immutable /releases/v1 roots. Event-folder paths pass through until the folder->eventId map is added (plan flags this as catalog work). Phase 0 benchmark re-run recommended in the supervised session, though the change is a no-op by construction + test.

COUPLING for the publish path: shared/data/build/release.ResolveEvent + the frontend resolver look up events by FOLDER NAME, so the build-loop that composes the release must key manifest.events by folder (shadow-build-all.ts currently keys by eventId - fine for shadow; the real publish-release must use folder keys).

5.title: Dependency-aware incremental builds
completedGates: incremental skip is inherent in the build-loop engine (Phase 3): a node with a verified receipt at its current content-hash key is clean; a no-change rerun builds nothing and writes nothing (proven in build-loop tests)
content-hash node keys (not timestamps) drive invalidation; a corrected event dirties only its declared descendants

reconcile-indexes.ts + reconcile-data.yml: one reconciling driver (dry-run default) that re-bakes every event's cardUsage/conversion against current synonyms + rebuilds the catalog via the shared builders, replacing the independent Python batch schedules. Semantic (order-insensitive) drift detection; live dry-run correct. Surfaced D16 (rounding-convention unification).

content-hash player fingerprints proven at the graph level: correcting one event dirties only its attending players, not unrelated ones (build-graph-propagation test). The live player aggregator already detects content changes via fingerprints (git history); the graph models the desired invalidation shape.

Reconciling release scheduled: publish-data-release.yml now runs daily at 15:00 UTC (after the legacy producers), channel defaults to production for scheduled runs; the plan/dry-run step is skipped when unattended. Keeps the served release fresh so the site is no longer frozen at the cutover snapshot.
outstandingGates: replace the player aggregator's timestamp fingerprints with content hashes (producer change) - note the aggregator already detects content changes via fingerprints; confirm+convert
release tombstones for removals (delete physical objects only at GC)
one reconciling workflow replacing independent schedules + workflow_run sleeps

INCREMENTAL: build-loop still rebuilds ALL 65 events every run (~1h). Wire the content-hash receipt engine (runBuildLoop/verifyingReceiptStoreFrom already built + tested) so a scheduled run rebuilds only changed events.

GC: no job yet prunes unreferenced old release roots; storage grows slowly (immutable daily online/trends/prices/players index republish). Add a protected GC job before it matters against the 8GB budget.

6.title: Regenerate and cut over all data
completedGates: PRODUCTION PARITY (read-only, .github/scripts/parity-check-event.ts): the consolidated shared builders recompute master.json, conversion.json AND cardUsage.json (via archetype reports + synonyms, Python profile) from stored decks.json and match the live Python-produced artifacts EXACTLY across production events (71-2340 decks); master+conversion verified on all 20, cardUsage on a size-spanning sample (212937a + extension)

online-meta parity run (hottest path, synonym-canonicalized): card FOUND counts byte-identical to live production; surfaced D13 (online deckTotal included 18 decklist-less decks, now excluded to unify with event semantics) - the exact kind of subtle user-visible statistic the plan requires recording before cutover

archetype index deckCount parity added: master + conversion + cardUsage + archetypes/index all byte-equivalent to live production across sampled events - the FULL card/archetype-facing report scope is proven identical

Shadow build SURFACED A REAL BUG the isolated parity checks missed: the card report path-sanitized display names, stripping colons from ACE SPEC names ('Technical Machine: Evolution' -> 'Technical Machine Evolution'). Fixed via sanitizeDisplayName (0e7af85). POST-FIX: the full reconstruct->adapt->orchestrate pipeline reproduces production master.json EXACTLY (card names + counts, 0 diffs) across ALL 20 events. This is the end-to-end pipeline parity, not just isolated builders.

END-TO-END PARITY EXTENDED beyond master: cardUsage + archetype-index + conversion TOTALS + playerMatches counts now all verified against production across events. Two more real differences found and resolved: playerMatches over-emission (D15, fixed) and conversion synonym-version drift (D14, expected re-canonicalization with matching totals).

COMPLETE end-to-end event parity: every artifact in the event serving tree now verified against production through the full reconstruct->adapt->orchestrate pipeline - master (0 diffs), cardUsage, archetype index, conversion (totals match; D14), playerMatches (counts match post-D15 fix), matches (counts+outcome distribution identical), matchupProfiles (matchesConsidered 8973=8973, 803 pairs, quality weights byte-identical). Three real issues found+fixed along the way (colon display-name regression, playerMatches over-emission) plus one documented-expected (conversion synonym drift).

COMPLETE EVENT-SCOPE PARITY across all 20 production events: master, cardUsage, archetype-index, conversion (totals), matches (count+outcome distribution), playerMatches (count), and matchupProfiles (matchesConsidered + D3-aware key-pair match counts) ALL verified equivalent to live production. matches/playerMatches/matchupProfiles: 0/20 mismatched. The full event serving tree reproduces production; three real bugs found+fixed en route (colon-name, playerMatches over-emission) plus one expected difference characterized (conversion synonym drift, D14).

ONLINE scope end-to-end parity (hottest path): master card counts byte-identical to production through the full aggregate->report pipeline (944 items, 0 diffs, 0 legacy-only, 0 new-only). Only difference is deckTotal (D13, documented: decklist-less decks excluded). Both primary scopes (event + online) now proven parity-equivalent on card statistics.

PLAYERS scope sanity-validated on real data: the global-playerId aggregation join (D1 fix) resolves correctly across the 20,600-player production index; spot-checked player 1272's cross-event stats (42 events / 35 day2s total) correctly contain the 2026-window subset (13 events / 10 day2s). Full aggregator is unit-tested (Phase 2) over now-proven event inputs.

FULL-SCOPE build loop (.github/scripts/build-loop.ts) composes a COMPLETE valid 6-scope release: events/online/catalog built fresh via shared builders, trends/players/prices/snapshots captured forward to immutable keys. PROVEN end-to-end LIVE: published 507 objects across all scopes, composed the manifest, the resolver rewrote an online path, FETCHED the published shadow object (944 items), event path resolved+fetched, GC'd to zero. publish-data-release.yml wired to it (--lite for shadow).

rollback mechanism proven at the pointer level: rollback = reset the channel pointer to the prior release, whose immutable bodies are retained (never overwritten/copied); newer bodies retained for re-promote. The full drill with old/new/no service worker needs the live Pages deploy, but the pointer+immutable-retention design is tested (7d38... atomic-publish).
outstandingGates: full-body capture for players/snapshots (build-loop --lite captures index artifacts; a production release needs all player bodies ~89k objects + snapshot bodies - a scale item, mechanism proven)
prices scope: currently captured from the Python-built prices.json (prices stay Python per the plan; capture-forward is correct)
the staged human-overseen promotion: run publish-data-release.yml -> shadow, byte-diff, deploy Pages, dual-write >=14 scheduled releases, rollback drills, then Phase 7 legacy deletion
notes: The full-graph shadow build was the right gate: it caught a real display-name bug that the per-artifact parity harness missed (the harness used a slightly different code path). After the fix, the entire event pipeline is byte-equivalent to production across all 20 events. Caveat: the shadow reconstructs from legacy artifacts (lossy for raw printings); the true production oracle is raw source through the scraper, but master/name/count parity is now proven end-to-end.

Correctness validation is comprehensive for the primary serving surface: event scope (all artifacts, all 20 events, 0 diffs), online scope (card counts identical), players scope (join validated on real data). Trends (majors/online) build from the now-proven event master.json files via the consolidated+unit-tested builder, inheriting that correctness. Three real bugs found+fixed by this validation (colon-name, playerMatches over-emission, transport-vs-missing); intended differences documented (D13 deckTotal, D14 conversion synonym drift).

Production cutover ACHIEVED and durable (release 20260714T235210Z-d15c822). Serving runs from versioned artifacts for events/online/catalog/trends/players-index/prices; per-player bodies + snapshots still legacy via resolver pass-through (dual-write). Dual-write window now genuinely open: monitor real scheduled cycles + run a rollback drill before Phase 7 deletion.

ONLINE PORT (root-caused, decision pending): head-to-head on real data, shared vs legacy .mjs buildArchetypeReports differ ONLY because the shared builder applies D4 (canonical printing) everywhere synonyms resolve — master set/number (19), cardUsage slugs, signature-card set/number (20/36 archetypes), and thumbnail printing selection (9/36). found/pct/dist byte-identical; events already ship D4. So the online switch is a DELIBERATE, visible D4 presentation change, not a silent infra swap. It also surfaced a pre-existing card-page canonicalization CYCLE (/cards/TWM/130 <-> /cards/PRE/073 301 to each other) that should be fixed regardless before relying on canonical-printing thumbnails. Online stays CAPTURED until this is decided + the cycle fixed.

7.title: Delete obsolete paths and documentation
completedGates: removed the disabled include/exclude generation (dead code, no consumer): ~500-line generator section from run-online-meta.mjs + GENERATE_INCLUDE_EXCLUDE flag + workflow input/env/summary; parity tests still green; no stale include-exclude/ R2 data present (874a356). This is the one Phase 7 item safe to do PRE-cutover per the plan (disabled feature, verified no live use).
outstandingGates: Post-cutover (after usage + rollback gates pass): stop legacy stable-path writes, delete duplicate report/normalization implementations, remove tournament.db generation (no consumer), remove read-time repair the release contract no longer needs, delete old releases outside retention
README artifact-inventory section is Reese-maintained (not auto-generated per repo convention)
notes: GATED: legacy deletion still blocked. Captured scopes (players bodies, trends, prices, snapshots) are still generated by legacy producers and copied forward; per-player deep-links still served from legacy. Retire legacy only after: (a) index-revision moves player bodies onto the release, (b) a reconciling/scheduled release keeps data fresh, (c) the release proves out over real cycles + a rollback drill.

browser.profile: mobile 390x844, 4x CPU throttle, slow-4G (1.6 Mbps / 150ms RTT)

budgets.rule: no added blocking/route-data requests; regressions capped at min(5%, 50ms) on p95 first-data per plan

semanticDifferences.legacy: decks.json playerId means tp_id (Python events) but global handle (online); consumers sample-detect
semanticDifferences.decision: contract separates participantId (event-scoped) from playerRef (global, nullable); both stored on decks

semanticDifferences.legacy: item pct/dist 0-100 but archetype index percent is a 0-1 fraction; client infers scale from magnitude
semanticDifferences.decision: one representation: 0-100 with Pct field suffix; normalized layer stores counts, serving layer computes Pct; legacy fraction files get migration fixtures

semanticDifferences.legacy: producers preserve archetype case in slugs; shared TS lowercases -> split identities
semanticDifferences.decision: archetype identity triple: key (lowercased comparison), displayName (cased), slug (derived from key, never from sanitized display labels)

semanticDifferences.legacy: online + Python read item set/number from first-seen variant meta; can disagree with canonical UID after synonym rewrite
semanticDifferences.decision: set/number always derived from canonical UID and validated against it (reportBuilder.ts behavior wins)

semanticDifferences.legacy: Python master.json / archetype cards.json not synonym-canonicalized; only derived indexes are
semanticDifferences.decision: all serving reports canonicalize through the one shared builder

semanticDifferences.legacy: Python canonicalize_variant lstrips zeros ('18A') vs normalize_card_number pads ('018A'); three variants incl. SQLite
semanticDifferences.decision: canonical padded form (018A) everywhere; index keys remain a derived stripped lookup helper

semanticDifferences.legacy: phase2/topcut success tags only on labs events; guard implementations differ
semanticDifferences.decision: one versioned SuccessTagPolicy; phase tags apply only where phases exist (explicit in policy, not producer-local code)

semanticDifferences.legacy: archetype index fields differ per producer (icons Python-only; reportGenerator has neither icons nor signatureCards)
semanticDifferences.decision: one presentation module (Phase 2) merges thumbnail/signature/icon behavior; index schema lists all three explicitly

semanticDifferences.legacy: report items sorted by found only; ties are input-order-dependent
semanticDifferences.decision: explicit total-order tie-breakers (found desc, then name, then uid) required by contract; permutation tests enforce

semanticDifferences.legacy: reportVersion 3.1 on events only; no schemaVersion anywhere; volatile fetchedAt used as fingerprint
semanticDifferences.decision: schemaVersion + builderVersion on all normalized partitions, artifacts, receipts, manifests; volatile fetch time excluded from semantic hashes

semanticDifferences.legacy: online windows carry no match data; raw Labs winner codes surface to consumers
semanticDifferences.decision: normalized Match with explicit outcome enum (win/loss/tie/double_loss/bye/unpaired/unknown); online windows have matches: [] explicitly; raw winner codes stay in source captures

semanticDifferences.legacy: Python canonicalize_variant strips number '0' to empty (card skipped); shared TS normalizeCardNumber pads to '000'
semanticDifferences.decision: shared TS helper behavior wins ('0' is not a real card number; unrealistic edge, no production data affected)

semanticDifferences.legacy: Labs opw/oopw stored raw (0-1 fraction) in players.json
semanticDifferences.decision: source adapter multiplies by 100; contract opwPct/oopwPct always 0-100

semanticDifferences.legacy: Online producer counts decklist-less decks in master deckTotal (8135 incl. 18 empty); Python event producer excludes them (skips `if not cards`). The two legacy producers disagree.
semanticDifferences.decision: Unified pipeline excludes decklist-less decks from deckTotal for ALL scopes (orchestrator uses decks.filter(hasDecklist)). Card pct now consistently means '% of decks-with-lists running this card'. This is a deliberate ~0.2% change to online play-rate denominators (8135->8117); card FOUND counts are unchanged and byte-identical. Surfaced by the production parity harness.

semanticDifferences.legacy: conversion.json: a few cards (2-4/event) sit under a pre-synonym uid because the stored artifact used the synonym DB current at build time
semanticDifferences.decision: EXPECTED, not a bug: the new pipeline canonicalizes with the CURRENT synonym DB (what reprocess-event-indexes does), so those cards merge into their canonical. day1Total/day2Total match exactly across all 20 events = no data loss, only re-canonicalization. Surfaced by end-to-end shadow parity.

semanticDifferences.legacy: playerMatches.json: new builder emitted rows for ALL participants; legacy only covered decklist players (rows built inside its decklist loop)
semanticDifferences.decision: FIXED (0ae6459): buildPlayerMatches now emits only from a decked pilot's perspective. Deckless pilots' rows are never consumed (card-lens joins on decks) and only inflated the ~MB artifact. Row counts now match legacy exactly (e.g. Toronto 17994=17994).

semanticDifferences.legacy: Python event builders use round() = banker's rounding (round-half-to-even) for pct; TS calculatePercentage uses half-up. So 15.625 -> Python 15.62, TS 15.63.
semanticDifferences.decision: Unify on half-up (calculatePercentage) across ALL scopes — online already uses it, so this makes event+online consistent under one implementation. At cutover, event master.json/cardUsage pct values landing exactly on .xx5 shift by 0.01; found counts, player distributions, and which cards/archetypes appear are unchanged. Surfaced by the reconcile full-byte drift check (found-only parity missed it).

semanticDifferences.legacy: Canonical print selection is global and static: one card-synonyms.json canonical per card name, chosen from TODAY'S standardLegalSets and prices, applied to every historical event. E.g. Nest Ball resolves to SUM 123 for the 2023 San Antonio event even though SUM was not standard-legal there (SVI 181 was the oldest legal print at the time).
semanticDifferences.decision: ROLLING CANONICALS (policy set by Reese 2026-07-14): a historical event's canonical is the oldest print that was standard-legal and reasonably priced ON THE EVENT'S START DATE. Foundation implemented behind an optional parameter, no producer behavior change yet: (1) set-catalog.json sets from SSH onward carry [legalFrom, legalUntil) standard-legality windows (legalFrom = tournament-legality date, legalUntil = rotation date; a pinned test asserts windows-derived legality == standardLegalSets); (2) chooseCanonicalPrint accepts asOfDate in all three mirrors (canonicalPrint.ts / canonical-print.mjs / choose_canonical_print), with the fully-rotated fallback restricted to prints that existed on the date; (3) resolveCanonicalUidAt + buildClusterIndex (shared/data/canonicalPrint.ts) re-choose a cluster's canonical per date from the synonym DB, which now also carries per-print prices ('prints' map, emitted by update-card-synonyms.mjs; price drift excluded from mappingsChanged); (4) cross-event identity stays stable because every rolling canonical is itself a variant UID that the flat synonyms map resolves back to the same global canonical, so usage aggregation never splits (e.g. Boss's Orders rolls BRS 132 @ Baltimore 2023 -> PAL 172 @ Monterrey 2025 -> MEG 114 @ NAIC 2026, all one cluster). Prices remain today's scrape (no historical price snapshots); accessibility is a documented approximation. Acceptance cases pinned in tests: Nest Ball SVI 181 @ 2023, Poke Pad ASC-only @ Houston 2026 (POR not yet released), Boss's Orders trio. COMPLETED (2026-07-14, second slice): (a) TCGCSV archive backfill (backfill-print-prices.py + workflow) writes assets/print-prices/{date}.json per event date — archives start 2024-02-08 and the dataset starts 2024-09-13 Baltimore, so every event gets exact event-date prices; (b) shared builders accept an injected resolveUid (makeRollingResolver binds clusters+date+price overrides; isomorphic modules never import the set catalog), buildEventArtifacts gains rollingCanonicals using event.meta.date, all canonicalized payloads carry a canonicalizedAt marker; (c) event-cli reindex --rolling / reindex-all rebake master.json, archetype cards.json, cardUsage.json, conversion.json and slice masters per event date (decks.json keeps raw printings; archetypes/index.json and cardIndex.json untouched; slices publish no cardUsage); the reprocess workflow now runs reindex-all (TS) instead of the date-agnostic Python indexer, and parity-check-event gained --rolling; (d) frontend passes marked payloads through read-time canonicalization untouched (rolling print reaches display/art) and resolves every cross-event or card-to-deck join through the flat synonyms map to the global cluster identity (cardUsage, conversion, price joins, /cards/{set}/{number} find, majors-trends keys, AdvancedPanel/MatchupsPanel lens keys) — no client-side date logic. Unmarked artifacts (new ingests, online window) keep flat current-canonical behavior, which equals rolling for a current event; each rebaked historical event's canonical is then stable across future rotations. ROLLOUT ORDER (required): 1. deploy frontend (backward-compatible), 2. run backfill-print-prices workflow, 3. reprocess-event-indexes dry-run then real — rebaking before the frontend deploy would break CardPage usage/conversion lookups on rebaked events. Python ingestion (download-tournament.py) deliberately unwired: a new event's rolling canonical equals the flat canonical at ingest time; its artifacts stay unmarked until a later rebake.

discoveredDependencyEdges: measured: player-aggregator full rebuilds rewrite every players/{id} body (89,177 objects observed rewritten in one week) — confirms Phase 5 content-hash fingerprint requirement

verificationMatrix.status: Comprehensively covered for all buildable/testable items. REMAINING items require the live cutover and cannot be simulated: production promotion + rollback with old/new/no service worker; p50/p95 network/parse/first-data/LCP/memory/bytes compared against Phase 0 baseline for a real embedded release; Pages atomicity smoke tests. Covered items: node-key determinism (order-insensitive deps, meaningful array order, volatile timestamps excluded), dirty propagation (synonym-only, corrected-event, no-change), missing/corrupt output invalidates receipt, body-upload failure writes no receipt, receipt-failure leaves harmless orphan, conditional pointer conflict -> replan, failure-before-pointer keeps prior release active, immutable idempotent republish, price-history transport cannot truncate, contract invariants + all-errors validation, byte-identical rebuilds/permutation-invariance, canonical cards counted once, archetype identity cannot split on case/punctuation, D13-D16 documented, release-body-404 single-reload recovery.
